title: Question: would Suppr AI Document Translation fit this resource list?
author: zjg678
body:
Hi, thanks for maintaining this resource list.

I wanted to ask whether Suppr AI Document Translation would be in scope here. AI document translation for PDFs and academic files with layout-preserving reading workflows.

Canonical source and docs:
- Source: https://github.com/WildDataX/PDFMathTranslate-vs-Suppr.ai-PDFtranslate
- Docs: https://github.com/WildDataX/PDFMathTranslate-vs-Suppr.ai-PDFtranslate#readme

Additional links:
- https://suppr.wilddata.cn/translate

If it fits the list, I can send a small PR in the format you prefer. If it is outside the scope, no worries.

Disclosure: I maintain this submission. The links above are official source/docs links only, with no affiliate, referral, or tracking URLs.

title: Add claudio: macOS voice dictation app in Swift
author: ayushkumar1808
body:
Repository: https://github.com/ayushkumar1808/claudio

claudio is a Swift-based macOS menu bar app for system-wide voice dictation. Uses AVFoundation for audio capture, URLSessionWebSocketTask for streaming, and ApplicationServices for text injection. MIT license.

Possible category: Apps / Productivity or User Interface

title: I'd like to suggest DynaMic, a macOS tool built with SwiftUI
author: enderten
body:

it's on my github and it's free!
also the app have some errors but son i'm gonna fix it!

title: Add Kviskr - Speech-to-text app using whisper.cpp + CoreML
author: andyarntsen-alt
body:
Kviskr is a macOS speech-to-text app built with Swift/SwiftUI using whisper.cpp with CoreML acceleration for local Norwegian speech recognition.

- NB-Whisper model (2.2% WER on Norwegian)
- Push-to-talk, works in all apps
- Apple Neural Engine acceleration
- Free, signed and notarized

https://kviskr.no
https://github.com/andyarntsen-alt/kviskr-downloads

title: FileShot.io – Cross-platform zero-knowledge file sharing (works on iOS/macOS)
author: FileShot
body:
Hi — I'd like to suggest **FileShot.io** for this list.

It's a zero-knowledge file sharing tool that works seamlessly in Safari on iOS and macOS. Files are AES-256-GCM encrypted client-side via the Web Crypto API before upload. Useful for Swift/iOS developers sharing builds, assets, or sensitive data.

- No account required
- MIT licensed, self-hostable
- Works in Safari on all Apple platforms
- Desktop app (Electron) also available

GitHub: https://github.com/FileShot/FileShotZKE
Site: https://fileshot.io

title: Add a better than all markdown render repo of the Swift. Add a currently high-quality Markdown rendering library that outperforms those common 2k–3k-star Swift Markdown renderer libraries, loads documents of any length instantly, and supports streaming AI-driven conversational interaction.

title: 💡 Add this project to awesome-italia-opensource
author: FabrizioCafolla
body:
I founded Italia Open-source, a community that aims to share and grow the Italian open-source world, but more generally to highlight Italian tech excellence.

Our website [italiaopensource.com](https://italiaopensource.com/) is based on open data that reside on the [awesome-italia-opensource](https://github.com/italia-opensource/awesome-italia-opensource) repository, you can add your own open-source project, community tech(s), and much more. All through a simple pull request, you can add your data.

Hey there. 
I wonder if this repository is still being supported, I see some issues without a reply from August and so on.
Please clarify 🙏

title: Swift Weekly Brief - A multi author curated newsletter - is dead and not getting updated
author: sokol8
body:
According to

```
[Issue #201](https://swiftweeklybrief.com/issue-201/) 22 Jun 2022
Written by: [Jeroen Leenarts](https://twitter.com/appforce1)

This is it. The final goodbye of Swift Weekly Brief.

We are now dormant for almost half a year and after a quick discussion Kristaps and I feel it is time to make good on our promise to delete the entire list’s data.

We loved having you as readers, but we love respecting your data even more.
```

Does it make sense to delete it from resources or somehow mark that it's not being updated/sent anymore?

title: Open Source Updates for Swift Projects - links yields error because Revue is shutdown
author: sokol8
body:
"Open Source Updates for Swift Projects" linking to https://www.getrevue.co/profile/ossp_updates/ doesn't work and yields the following error "We’ve made the difficult decision to shut down Revue on January 18, 2023."

Shall the link be changed to https://ossp-updates.beehiiv.com/ ?

Please confirm and I will make a pull request if needed.

title: Add SVG category
author: f3dm76
body: Thought that SVG could be a separate category. I saw a library here that converts SVG images to UIImages in image category, but this library converts every SVG element to SwiftUI elements, so I didn't find a category for it. And seeing as PDF has its own category I decided to go with a separate category for SVG as well. Also contribution guide says to create issue in case I want new category, but should I open a PR in that case as well or just create the issue? I created a PR just in case https://github.com/matteocrippa/awesome-swift/pull/1728. Have a great day!
